dataType.description: Type vocabulary. Each entry maps 1:1 to an Apache Arrow DataType used by DataFusion; the dialect unparser is responsible for printing per-warehouse SQL. Composite/array types are intentionally absent until needed.

innerColumnRef.description: Reference to a column on one of the model's inner tables. Form 'alias.column' where alias is one of models[].tables[].alias and column is a column produced by that inner table.

tableReference.description: Physical address of an inner table inside the owning source.

innerTableJoin.description: How this inner table joins onto another inner table in the same model. Absent only on the model's single master table (the join root).

to.description: alias of another inner table in this model. Must not be self.

type.description: Join kind. v0 supports inner and left only — right joins are expressed as a left join with the operands swapped.

on.description: Equality conditions; multi-column joins use multiple entries (all ANDed). 'left' names a column on this inner table; 'right' names a column on the table referenced by 'to'.

innerTable.description: One physical input to a model. Either a tableReference (a real warehouse table) or a refSql (a warehouse-native SQL snippet inlined as a CTE during unparse). The set of inner tables forms a join graph rooted at the single master entry (the one without 'join').

alias.description: Identifier used inside this model (column.from and join.to reference it). Unique within models[].tables[]. Not exposed outside the model.

priority.description: Lower number = higher precedence. Used as a console-side hint when authoring columns whose name appears on multiple inner tables; the actual SQL output is decided strictly by column.from (no implicit COALESCE).

refSql.description: Warehouse-native SQL that defines this inner table when semantic SQL can't express it (e.g. dialect-specific functions like BigQuery QUALIFY, ML.PREDICT). Inlined as a CTE-equivalent during unparse to the owning source's dialect; DataFusion treats it opaquely. Mutually exclusive with tableReference. The columns it produces must match what is referenced by column.from — mcp does not introspect the statement.

from.description: Source of this column inside the model's inner tables. A single 'alias.col' maps 1:1. An array of 'alias.col' entries is emitted as COALESCE(...) in the given order — explicit, not derived from inner-table priority. Each alias must exist in models[].tables[].alias.

expression.description: Semantic-SQL scalar expression. May reference sibling columns and dotted-path relations (e.g. customer.country).

to.description: Target model name (must exist in models[].name).

via.description: Relationship name (must exist in relationships[].name) connecting this model to 'to'.

catalog.description: Default catalog name surfaced to semantic SQL (e.g. 'nl2sql'). Used as DataFusion SessionContext default catalog.

schema.description: Default schema name surfaced to semantic SQL (e.g. 'models').

sources.description: Warehouse sources. v0 routes each query to exactly one source; cross-source JOIN is rejected (cross_source_join error). See mcp/DESIGN.md §4.5.

type.description: Warehouse driver type. Phase 0 picks one target first; others light up as drivers land.

connectionRef.description: Indirect credential reference. Format 'secret:<name>' (k8s Secret) or 'env:<NAME>' (env var). Plaintext credentials in metadata are forbidden (ARCHITECTURE.md §1.2).

readOnlyRole.description: Optional warehouse role/user override. The infra contract requires this be read-only regardless.

federates.description: Optional federation declaration. If set, this source can route queries that span the listed sources (e.g. Trino in front of Postgres + BigQuery). v0 mcp ignores this field and rejects cross-source queries; Phase 3+ enables route_source fallback through a federation source. Object form (not bare strings) reserves room for dialect-specific mappings (remoteCatalog, etc.) to be added later without a schema migration.

source.description: Underlying source name. Must match one of sources[].name.

functions.description: MDL-declared functions on top of mcp's code-side baseline (Phase 3.9). Each entry stands in as a signature-only UDF so semantic SQL referencing the function passes DataFusion's planner; the routed-source whitelist (mcp's enforce_function_whitelist) then decides accept/reject. v0 stores the declarations; UDF registration + per-source enforcement activate when the first MDL declares one.

name.description: Function name as it appears in semantic SQL.

kind.description: DataFusion UDF family the registration will use.

returns.description: Declared return type (from the dataType vocabulary).

name.description: Globally unique model name. Referenced in semantic SQL as <catalog>.<schema>.<name> or short <name>.

source.description: Owning source. Must match one of sources[].name. A query touching models from multiple sources is rejected in v0; see federation note on sources[].federates.

description.description: Human description, used by search_tables ranking and shown in the console.

tables.description: Inner tables that compose the model. Exactly one entry must omit 'join' — that entry is the master (join root); all others must declare 'join.to' pointing at another alias in this same array. The join graph must be acyclic and connected. Cross-table references in 'join.on' and in column.from use aliases declared here.

columns.description: Externally visible columns. Names are the model's contract; physical kind columns are bound to inner tables via 'from'. Calculated and relation columns reference other externally visible column names — never inner aliases.

primaryKey.description: Physical column name(s) forming the model PK, in key order. A single column may be a string; composite keys use a non-empty string array. Required when another model targets this one via a relation.

name.description: Globally unique relationship name. Referenced by column kind='relation' via this name.

joinType.description: Cardinality from 'from' to 'to'. many_to_many is intentionally absent in v0 (model an explicit junction model instead).

name.description: Globally unique view name. Usable in semantic SQL as a relation alongside models.

statement.description: Semantic SQL statement. Inlined by the analyzer when the view is referenced.

policies.description: Row-filter / column-mask access-control bindings (Phase 3.8). Multiple matching policies for the same (caller, model) are AND-merged: row filters conjuncted, masked-column sets unioned.

principals.description: Match strings against the calling identity. Empty list means 'applies to anyone with identity'. 'alice@example.com' matches the caller email; 'group:foo' matches a caller group named foo.

rowFilter.description: SQL boolean expression evaluated against the bound model's non-relation columns. ANDed with the user query's WHERE clause.

maskedColumns.description: Names of non-relation columns on the bound model whose values must be replaced with NULL in the projection.
